name: Gold Rush Centennial Cash for Gold, Cash for Silver, Cash for Diamonds
description_short: Gold Rush buys gold, silver, platinum, diamonds, and estate jewelry at 7 Colorado locations, paying spot-price-based cash using certified scales and XRF purity analysis.
description_long:
Gold Rush, operating as Colorado Gold & Precious Metals LLC, was founded on January 6, 2011 and incorporated January 21, 2011 in Denver, Colorado. From a single location, the company expanded to seven retail stores across the Denver metro area and Colorado Springs. They have been BBB Accredited since October 15, 2012, giving them over a decade of documented business history. Their Centennial location at 7500 S University Blvd, Suite 109 operates Monday through Saturday, 9 AM to 6 PM, serving the south Denver suburbs.

Gold Rush's business model is simple: customers bring in unwanted or inherited precious metals and jewelry, Gold Rush evaluates them on-site, and pays immediately in cash or via wire transfer. They purchase gold in all forms — jewelry, coins, bullion, and dental gold — along with silver flatware, coins and bullion, platinum, palladium, diamonds, estate jewelry, and collectible coins. Pricing is tied to current precious metals spot prices, with weight measured on certified precision scales accurate to the tenth of a gram, and metal purity determined by professional-grade XRF (X-ray fluorescence) analysis. The full transaction typically completes in under 20 minutes.

The use of XRF technology is a meaningful differentiator in the gold-buying market. Unlike acid testing, XRF is entirely non-destructive and is performed openly in front of the customer, giving sellers direct visibility into how their items are being assessed. Paired with a no-obligation appraisal policy — customers can walk away after evaluation without any obligation to sell — this creates a relatively transparent buying environment. Gold Rush's Google rating of 5.0 from over 1,254 reviews reflects an unusually strong consumer satisfaction record for this industry, and their seven-location footprint gives them meaningful reach across Colorado's Front Range.

For Colorado residents looking to liquidate unwanted precious metals or diamonds, Gold Rush offers a well-established, transparent in-person process backed by solid technology and a long operating history. The primary limitations are geographic — they have no presence outside Colorado — and operational: there is no online quote tool, mobile app, or remote valuation service, so every transaction requires a physical store visit. One documented BBB complaint involving a significant discrepancy between an initial quote ($22,200) and a revised final offer ($16,320) on a gold coin transaction is worth flagging for sellers bringing in high-value collections, who should confirm all terms before completing a sale.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee on completed transactions. All evaluations are no-obligation — customers may decline to sell after appraisal at no cost.

pros: Uses professional-grade XRF (X-ray fluorescence) technology for non-destructive purity testing performed visibly in front of the customer
No-obligation appraisal policy — customers can have items evaluated and walk away with zero pressure to sell
5.0 Google rating from 1,254+ reviews — exceptionally strong satisfaction record for a precious metals buyer
BBB Accredited since October 2012 with over 13 years of documented operating history
7 store locations across Colorado's Front Range (Denver metro and Colorado Springs) for regional accessibility
Certified precision scales accurate to the tenth of a gram ensure weight-based pricing is exact
Transactions typically completed in under 20 minutes with same-day cash or wire transfer payment
cons: Colorado-only presence — no locations or services available to sellers outside the state
No online quote tool, remote valuation, or mobile app — every transaction requires an in-person store visit
One documented BBB complaint involves a substantial pricing discrepancy: a customer was quoted $22,200 for gold coins but received a revised offer of $16,320 at transaction time
BBB letter grade is not publicly confirmed in available sources
No ability to lock in or pre-confirm a price before visiting a store, as offers depend on real-time spot prices and in-person XRF results
best_for: Colorado residents with inherited gold jewelry, coins, or bullion they want to convert to immediate cash
Individuals liquidating silver flatware, dental gold, estate jewelry, or diamond pieces from an estate
Sellers who prioritize process transparency and want to observe their items being tested in real time
Local consumers who prefer an established multi-location buyer over mail-in gold services or pawnshop transactions
diagnosis: Gold Rush is best suited for Colorado-based individuals who want to sell unwanted precious metals or diamonds to a transparent, technology-equipped buyer with a strong local reputation and no-pressure evaluation process. The main caveat is that their service is entirely in-person with no digital tools, and sellers with high-value collections should carefully confirm the final offer before completing any transaction given the documented pricing discrepancy in their BBB complaint history.
services: Gold buying — jewelry, coins, bullion, dental gold
Silver buying — flatware, coins, jewelry, bullion
XRF (X-ray fluorescence) non-destructive purity analysis performed in-store
Precision weight measurement on certified scales (accurate to 0.1 gram)
Immediate cash payment at time of transaction
No-obligation in-store appraisal with no purchase requirement
